verification_gate: Four-system research (.kb GitKB, .meta meta, .handoff handoff, .idd rusty-idd) is runtime-proven; the .idd unification plan is landed in an existing repo planning surface with archive-first handling; the GitKB docs reference set (19/19 pages) is created clean in the operator-designated Drive folder with originals untouched.
proof_summary: Equation '.kb + .meta + .handoff + .idd -> .idd' planned, not yet executed. Decisive findings: git-kb 0.2.12 (= latest release v0.2.12 2026-06-06; closed-source binary, open protocol) stores canonically as TEXT (store/documents/*.md + commits/*.json) with derived SQLite cache; the .kb directory name is HARDCODED (GITKB_ROOT accepts only a directory containing .kb/) but a '.kb -> .idd/kb' symlink serves the full CLI (proven with a committed document); export/backup/bundle proven, no data lock-in. handoff's ledger is redb (magic bytes 'redb'), not SQLite; committed truth is ledger.events.jsonl per ADR-0004 SS3/ADR-0018 D1; live violation found: teri/.handoff/ledger.db.rvf is git-tracked. FlexNetOS/meta is a fork of gitkb/meta (vendor lineage; installed 0.2.22); GitKB documents a native '[repos] strategy=meta' integration. rusty-idd (v0.1.0, IDD = Intent-Driven Development) already defines the dot-directory authority model this plan extends; its knowledge index is stale (old /home/drdave workspace_root); hf and rusty-idd binaries are NOT installed (migration phase-0 gate). Plan: per-repo .idd/{kb,handoff,goals,knowledge,evidence,proofs,meta-references} with compatibility symlinks, text-canonical-journal storage rule (enforcing existing ADRs), 4-phase strangler migration with count+sha256 parity proofs and rollbacks. Docs polish: all 19 official gitkb.com docs pages recreated as clean stamped Google Docs with Local Notes (runtime deltas incl. nonexistent 'config set', [user]-not-[identity] author key, conflict verbs, 0.2.6-stale CLI page); originals + 7 vendor zips left untouched in place.
